title: Recurrent-Event Frailty Specialist for Driver/Race Heterogeneity
summary: Pit stops are repeated events, and recurrent-event literature emphasizes subject-specific heterogeneity and time-varying effects that plain global classifiers miss.
rationale: Pit stops are repeated events, and recurrent-event literature emphasizes subject-specific heterogeneity and time-varying effects that plain global classifiers miss. In this dataset, drivers, races, and compounds plausibly have different latent stop propensities even after controlling for tyre age and race progress.
implementation_hint: Train a discrete-time hazard or mixed-effects logistic specialist with spline-like terms for `TyreLife`, `RaceProgress`, and degradation, plus shrinkage random effects or cross-fitted empirical-Bayes encodings for `Driver`, `Race`, and `Compound x Stint`. Use it as an orthogonal model family and blend its OOF probabilities.
expected_effect: Improved handling of rare drivers/races and repeated-stop behavior, especially where the main model currently uses only frequency encodings.
risk: Mixed-effects fitting can be slower and brittle at this scale, and unseen IDs need strong shrinkage defaults to avoid noisy extrapolation.
